sourceMovie.title: Primal Fear
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Primal Fear (1996) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: A Walk Among the Tombstones
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Primal Fear clicked for you, A Walk Among the Tombstones (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Primal Fear?
faq.answer: Try A Walk Among the Tombstones, The Outfit, The Lady from Shanghai—each shares DNA with Primal Fear while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Primal Fear?
faq.answer: Primal Fear (1996) is best described as Crime, Drama, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Primal Fear worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
